title: Public Health Principles and Practice
catalogDescription: Provides a broad overview of public health and introduces foundational principles, theories, and frameworks of public health and key issues faced today. Course overlaps with: TBIOMD 201. Offered: AWSp.

title: Science and Public Health
catalogDescription: Provide an overview and introduction to the ways different scientific disciplines are used to address public health goals. Course overlaps with: TBIOMD 201. Prerequisite: minimum grade of 1.7 in SPH 380; either BIOL 118, BIOL 180, CHEM 120, CHEM 142, CHEM 145, or both MICROM 301 and MICROM 302. Offered: AWSp.
prerequisiteNotes: Official UW prerequisite text: minimum grade of 1.7 in SPH 380; either BIOL 118, BIOL 180, CHEM 120, CHEM 142, CHEM 145, or both MICROM 301 and MICROM 302.

title: Research Methods in Public Health
catalogDescription: Applies concepts and research methods to real public health problems. Prerequisite: EPI 320; a minimum grade of 1.7 in SPH 381; and a minimum grade of 1.7 in SPH 389. Offered: AWSp.
